commands.repair_reason: Contributor live proof and maintainer security-policy approval are needed; there is no narrow automated repair from this pass.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain, dependency, workflow, secret-handling, or permission-broadening regression was found in the diff; the remaining security issue is proof and policy validation of the intended boundary. (sha=433bc658436c64870c1a2680f859e049dfbaf5e3)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Convex package sanction batches, package soft-delete reason metadata, user ban/delete/unban hooks, regression tests, changelog, and moderation spec updates for package/plugin resources owned by sanctioned personal accounts.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main's user ban/delete paths do not touch package rows or package publish tokens, while the PR adds explicit package batches and focused regression coverage.
commands.review_followup: Contributor live proof and maintainer security-policy approval are needed; there is no narrow automated repair from this pass.

target.title: fix: hide package resources when owners are banned
target.body:
## Summary

Makes package/plugin resources follow personal account sanctions without crossing org ownership boundaries.

## What changed

- Personal/no-publisher packages and releases are hidden when their owner is banned or deletes their account.
- Package publish tokens for those personal rows are revoked during ban/account deletion.
- `packages.softDeletedReason` distinguishes ban-hidden packages from account-deleted packages.
- Unban restore only restores packages hidden by the matching ban.
- Org-owned packages are preserved even when a banned user remains in a legacy `ownerUserId` field.

## Behavior proof

The focused owned-package sanction suite proves the intended split:

- Ban/delete hides personal package resources and revokes publish tokens.
- Unban restores only matching ban-hidden packages.
- Account-deleted packages are not restored by unban.
- Org-owned packages are not hidden just because one member or legacy owner user is banned.

Policy note for maintainer review: this PR intentionally treats personal package resources as sanction-bound while preserving org-owned resources.

## Validation

```text
$ bun run test convex/packages.public.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       138 passed (138)
```

```text
$ bun run ci:static
passed
```

```text
$ bun run ci:unit
Test Files  204 passed (204)
Tests       1994 passed (1994)
Statements  85.98%
Branches    75.17%
Functions   86.37%
Lines        89.48%
```

```text
$ bun run ci:types-build
tsc, package typechecks, clawhub-mod typecheck, Vite build, and Nitro build passed.
```

## External blocker

The known Vercel preview failure is team authorization required for deployment. That is maintainer-owned and not a patch failure.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff, can maintain it, and validated it with the commands above.
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski
